examples.title: Order of Operations
examples.description: A pseudocode example demonstrating the order of operations in Python.
examples.explanation: This pseudocode example demonstrates the order of operations in Python. The expression 5 + 3 * 2 is evaluated as 5 + (3 * 2) = 5 + 6 = 11.

examples.title: Comparison Operators
examples.description: A Python example demonstrating the comparison operators.
examples.code:
x = 5
y = 3
print(x > y)
print(x == y)
print(x != y)
examples.explanation: This Python example demonstrates the comparison operators. The expressions x > y, x == y, and x != y are evaluated as True, False, and True respectively.

examples.title: Logical Operators
examples.description: A Python example demonstrating the logical operators.
examples.code:
x = True
y = False
print(x and y)
print(x or y)
print(not x)
examples.explanation: This Python example demonstrates the logical operators. The expressions x and y, x or y, and not x are evaluated as False, True, and False respectively.
